text: Writing clean code is a skill that makes life easier for you and other developers. The most important goal is readable code, which means anyone can understand what your program does without long explanations. Good naming conventions are essential here: use clear names for variables and functions, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. Speaking of functions, each one should do only one specific thing. This makes your code modular and easier to test. Finally, think about maintainability. Clean code is simple, well-organized, and easy to update later. It saves time and reduces mistakes. Remember, you write code for humans to read, not just for computers.
